name: SQLi Filter Bypass (Advanced)
story: A development team rushed a mission lookup portal into production after adding a blacklist that they believed would stop SQL injection. The filter blocks a few lowercase tokens, but the backend still concatenates the request parameter directly into a SQL query. Your job is to study the endpoint, understand why case-sensitive filtering is not a defense, bypass the blacklist with uppercase SQL, and extract the hidden flag from the internal secrets table.
objectives: Understand filter weaknesses, bypass token checks, and exfiltrate the flag via SQL injection.

steps.title: Locate the vulnerable parameter
steps.description: Inspect the mission lookup endpoint and identify the injectable parameter.
steps.question: Which query parameter is used directly in SQL without parameterization?

hints.text: The base query uses WHERE id = <input>.

steps.title: Bypass the naive filter
steps.description: The application blocks lowercase SQL tokens only. Bypass this weak check.
steps.question: Which uppercase SQL clause can bypass the lowercase-only blacklist?

hints.text: Try the same payload with uppercase keywords.

steps.title: Extract the flag
steps.description: After bypassing the filter, union data from the secrets table and capture the flag.
steps.question: What is the captured flag?

hints.text: The endpoint returns three columns: id, name, difficulty.
